title: Interpret AGB HV Charge Percentage During Extended Delay Or Troubleshooting
summary: Use the HV charge percentage shown in the selected AGB power section as an operational awareness reference during prolonged idle time or extended troubleshooting. The training source indicates 50% as an example displayed value and identifies roughly 30% to 32% as edging toward concern when the AGB has been sitting for hours or issue resolution is taking one to two hours.
when_to_use: Use when an AGB is selected in the interface and support needs to interpret the displayed HV charge percentage during extended delay, prolonged idle time, or troubleshooting that may take significant time.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a formal shutdown threshold procedure.
Do not use this runbook to determine a charging threshold or mandatory corrective action, because the source does not define one.
Do not use this runbook as a recovery procedure for moving, restarting, or charging the AGB.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Select the AGB, view the power section, read the displayed HV charge percentage, and interpret whether extended delay conditions increase operational concern based on the training guidance.

safety_notes: This source provides operational awareness guidance only, not a formal battery control procedure.
Do not invent a shutdown threshold, charging threshold, or corrective action because the source does not provide one.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the AGB selection interface
Visibility of the power section and HV charge percentage
Awareness of elapsed idle or troubleshooting time

access_required: Access to the AGB selection interface
system_preconditions: An AGB can be selected in the interface.
The power section showing HV charge percentage is visible for the selected AGB.

visual_references.description: Training video frame associated with the discussion of AGB HV charge percentage, extended idle time, and low-charge concern during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Select the AGB and view the power section
steps.purpose: Locate the interface area where the AGB HV charge percentage is displayed.
steps.instruction: Select the AGB in the interface and open or view the small section labeled power so the HV charge percentage is visible.
steps.expected_result: The selected AGB shows a power section with an HV charge percentage.
steps.healthy_condition: The power section is visible and the HV charge percentage can be read.
steps.failure_condition: The AGB cannot be selected or the power section / HV charge percentage is not visible.

actions.label: Select AGB
actions.exact_action: Select the AGB in the interface.

actions.label: View power section
actions.exact_action: View the small section that says power and identify the HV charge percentage.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The selected AGB detail area and the referenced power section where HV charge percentage is discussed.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the HV charge percentage cannot be viewed from the selected AGB interface.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you select the AGB, there's a small section over here that says power; it will tell you the charge percentage of the HV. So it's at 50%.

steps.title: Read the current HV charge percentage
steps.purpose: Capture the current displayed HV value for interpretation.
steps.instruction: Read the current HV charge percentage displayed in the power section for the selected AGB.
steps.expected_result: A current HV charge percentage is identified for the selected AGB.
steps.healthy_condition: A numeric percentage is displayed and readable.
steps.failure_condition: No readable HV charge percentage is displayed.

actions.label: Read HV percentage
actions.exact_action: Read the current HV charge percentage displayed for the AGB.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The power section value referenced in training, including the example showing 50%.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the displayed HV percentage is unavailable or unreadable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you select the AGB, there's a small section over here that says power; it will tell you the charge percentage of the HV. So it's at 50%.

steps.title: Compare the displayed value to the training examples
steps.purpose: Use the source-provided example values to interpret whether the current reading appears comfortably above or closer to concern during delay.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed HV charge percentage to the training examples: 50% is given as an example displayed reading, while 30% to 32% is described as edging toward concern when delays continue.
steps.expected_result: The current reading is categorized relative to the example values discussed in training.
steps.healthy_condition: The observed value is clearly understood in relation to the example values from the source.
steps.failure_condition: The user attempts to apply unsupported thresholds or conclusions beyond the source examples.

actions.label: Compare to examples
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed value to the example values discussed in training, including 50% as an example current reading and 30% to 32% as values called out as edging toward concern during long issue resolution.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training context tied to the HV percentage discussion and example values.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if interpretation depends on a formal threshold not stated in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you select the AGB, there's a small section over here that says power; it will tell you the charge percentage of the HV. So it's at 50%.

It sat there for hours waiting to be tipped on a tipper and it died. If you're already working with something that is like 30%, 32%, and it takes an hour or two to fix the issue, you're edging towards that area.

steps.title: Account for extended idle or troubleshooting time
steps.purpose: Apply the source condition that elapsed delay time increases concern for lower HV values.
steps.instruction: Consider whether the AGB has been sitting for hours or whether issue resolution is taking an hour or two, because the training source states those delays increase concern about the remaining HV charge.
steps.expected_result: The HV reading is interpreted in the context of elapsed idle or troubleshooting time.
steps.healthy_condition: The reading is evaluated together with the known delay duration.
steps.failure_condition: Delay duration is ignored even though the source specifically ties concern to hours of waiting or one to two hours of issue resolution.

actions.label: Assess delay duration
actions.exact_action: Consider whether the AGB has been sitting for hours or whether issue resolution is taking an hour or two.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training frame associated with the discussion of an AGB sitting for hours and low-30% HV during troubleshooting.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the issue remains unresolved while the HV percentage continues to trend toward the low values discussed in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: It sat there for hours waiting to be tipped on a tipper and it died. If you're already working with something that is like 30%, 32%, and it takes an hour or two to fix the issue, you're edging towards that area.

steps.title: Record approaching low-charge operational concern
steps.purpose: Document the source-supported interpretation when low-30% HV is observed during extended delay conditions.
steps.instruction: Record that the AGB is approaching a low-charge operational concern if the displayed percentage is in the low-30% range during extended delay conditions.
steps.expected_result: The AGB status is documented as approaching a low-charge operational concern when the source-supported conditions are met.
steps.healthy_condition: The interpretation is recorded as an operational concern only, without adding unsupported thresholds or actions.
steps.failure_condition: The record states a formal shutdown point, charging mandate, or corrective action not supported by the source.

actions.label: Record concern status
actions.exact_action: Record that the AGB is approaching a low-charge operational concern if the displayed percentage is in the low-30% range during extended delay conditions.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The source segment supporting low-30% HV as edging toward concern during long delays.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the AGB cannot be moved.
Escalate if the issue remains unresolved while the HV percentage continues to trend toward the low values discussed in the source.
Do not proceed by inventing a shutdown threshold, charging threshold, or corrective action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: It sat there for hours waiting to be tipped on a tipper and it died. If you're already working with something that is like 30%, 32%, and it takes an hour or two to fix the issue, you're edging towards that area.

success_criteria: The selected AGB HV charge percentage can be viewed in the power section.
The current HV percentage is interpreted using only the source-provided examples and delay context.
Low-30% readings during extended delay conditions are recognized as approaching operational concern without inventing unsupported thresholds or actions.
healthy_conditions: The AGB power section is visible and shows an HV charge percentage.
The displayed value can be read and compared to the training examples.
The AGB is not yet in the low-30% concern context described by the source, or delay conditions do not indicate increasing concern.
failure_conditions: The AGB cannot be selected or the power section cannot be viewed.
The HV charge percentage is not readable.
The issue remains unresolved while the HV percentage continues to trend toward the low values discussed in the source.
The user attempts to apply unsupported shutdown thresholds, charging thresholds, or corrective actions.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the AGB cannot be moved.
Escalate if the issue remains unresolved while the HV percentage continues to trend toward the low values discussed in the source.
Escalate rather than inventing a shutdown threshold, charging threshold, or corrective action not provided by the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training frame associated with the AGB power section and extended-delay HV charge discussion.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: It sat there for hours waiting to be tipped on a tipper and it died. If you're already working with something that is like 30%, 32%, and it takes an hour or two to fix the issue, you're edging towards that area.
If you select the AGB, there's a small section over here that says power; it will tell you the charge percentage of the HV. So it's at 50%.

missing_details: The source does not define a formal shutdown threshold for HV charge in this segment.
The source does not define a mandatory charging threshold in this segment.
The source does not provide a required corrective action when low-30% HV is observed during delay.
The source does not specify how to record the concern in a system or log.
The supplied artifact is only indirectly aligned to the HV discussion and does not clearly isolate the power section UI.
